title: Lucknow Becomes Capital
focus: Historical context of the Awadh capital shift by Nawab Asaf-ud-Daula
rationale: This section establishes the foundational timeline and the key historical figure responsible for making Lucknow the cultural and political center of Awadh.
sectionExcerpt: Nawab Asaf-ud-Daula (1775 to 1797) moved the capital of Awadh from Faizabad to Lucknow in 1775. This decision marked the beginning of a cultural revival in the city.
evidenceBullets: The capital of Awadh was moved from Faizabad to Lucknow in the year 1775.

title: Architectural Splendour of Asaf-ud-Daula's Era
focus: Key architectural monuments of Lucknow including Bara Imambara and Rumi Darwaza
rationale: This section covers highly examinable architectural features of Lucknow's monuments, directly linked to a UPSC 2018 PYQ regarding the materials used in their construction.
sectionExcerpt: During Asaf-ud-Daula's era, magnificent monuments were constructed. The Bara Imambara features one of the largest arched halls without support beams. The Rumi Darwaza is an imposing gateway inspired by Istanbul's architecture. A 2018 UPSC PYQ tests knowledge on whether red sandstone and marble were used in making these monuments.
evidenceBullets: Bara Imambara features one of the largest arched halls built without support beams.
Rumi Darwaza is an imposing gateway inspired by the architecture of Istanbul.
UPSC 2018 PYQ specifically tested the building materials (red sandstone and marble) used in Bara Imambara and Rumi Darwaza.
